chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. C.P No. D-7430 of 2022 _____________________________________________________________ Dated Order with signature of Judge. _____________________________________________________________ Fresh case 1. For order on Misc. No.31481/2022 2. For order on Misc. No.31482/2022 3. For order on Misc. No.31483/2022 4. For hearing of main case. 05.12.2022. Mr. Sh. M. Suleman, Advocate for the Petitioner --------------- YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J.- The Petitioner has invoked the jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution, seeking that action be intitated in the matter of certain tenders said to have been awarded by Respondents Nos. 1 and 2. Paradoxically, the prayers advanced seeks to include the Respondent No.1 itself as one of the parties to whom directions are sought to be issued for initiation of such action. Furthermore, the Petition is completely vague, in as much as it is bereft of any material particulars, with it merely having been pleaded that the Respondents Nos. 1 and 2 have awarded contracts worth Rs.360 million to their favorite contractors during the month of October, 2022 without publication of tenders in any daily newspaper, on the pretext that such a process could not followed on account of it being a rainy season. However, no detail of even a single such contract or contractor has been provided, and no reference has been made to any attempt on the part of the Petitioner to raise the matter before the Sindh Public Procurement Regulatory Authority. Under the given circumstances, we are of the view that the matter does not present a fit case for issuance of writ, hence while granting the application for urgency we hereby dismiss the same in limine along with the other pending miscellaneous applications, with costs of Rs. 10,000/- to be deposited by the Petitioner towards the Prime Minister’s Flood Relief Fund within seven days and the receipt submitted before the office.

However, no detail of even a single such contract or contractor has been provided, and no reference has been made to any attempt on the part of the Petitioner to raise the matter before the Sindh Public Procurement Regulatory Authority. Under the given circumstances, we are of the view that the matter does not present a fit case for issuance of writ, hence while granting the application for urgency we hereby dismiss the same in limine along with the other pending miscellaneous applications, with costs of Rs. 10,000/- to be deposited by the Petitioner towards the Prime Minister’s Flood Relief Fund within seven days and the receipt submitted before the office. JUDGE CHIEF JUSTICE TariqAli/PA
